hero.title: Bridgex
hero.subtitle: Your Bridge to Markdown
hero.description: An open-source graphical interface for converting files to Markdown, built in Python with PySide6. Simplify your workflow with the power of Markitdown.

crossPlatform.title: Cross-Platform
crossPlatform.description: Works on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Use the GUI on any system.

multiFormat.title: Multi-Format Support
multiFormat.description: Convert PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, ePub, and many more formats to Markdown.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: Fully open source under MIT license. Inspect, modify, and redistribute freely.

lightEditor.title: Light Editor
lightEditor.description: Built-in lightweight editor to review and tweak your converted Markdown before saving.

multilingual.title: Multilingual
multilingual.description: Interface available in multiple languages: English, Spanish, and more.

modular.title: Modular Design
modular.description: Easy to adapt and extend. The entire UI is built with Qt Designer files.

windows.title: Windows
windows.description: Download the installer (.exe) for Windows 64-bit. Includes all dependencies.
windows.note: Windows 10/11 64-bit required. Not recommended for 32-bit systems.

linux.title: Linux / macOS
linux.description: Install via pip from PyPI. Python 3.9-3.13 required.
linux.note: Use a virtual environment for best results.

pip.title: Installation via pip
pip.step2: Create and activate a virtual environment (recommended):

windows.title: Windows Installation
windows.step1: Download the installer from the Downloads page
windows.step4: Bridgex will be installed in Program Files

vcredist.title: VC++ Runtime Error (Windows)
vcredist.description: If you see an error about missing Visual C++ Runtime, the installer will automatically install VC_Redist x64. If issues persist, download it manually from Microsoft.

permissions.title: Permission Denied (Windows)
permissions.description: Run the installer and application as Administrator. The program needs write access to certain folders.

pyside.title: PySide6 Installation Issues (Linux)
pyside.description: On some Linux distributions, you may need to install additional Qt dependencies. Run: sudo apt-get install libxcb-xinerama0 libxcb-cursor0

display.title: Display Issues (Linux)
display.description: If the GUI doesn't appear or looks broken, ensure you have a display server running (X11 or Wayland). For WSL, install an X server like VcXsrv.

usage.title: Basic Usage
usage.step1: Launch Bridgex from terminal or the installed shortcut
usage.step2: Click the upload button to select a file to convert
usage.step3: The file will be automatically converted to Markdown
usage.step4: Review and edit the result in the built-in editor
usage.step5: Click save to export your Markdown file

about.title: About Bridgex
about.subtitle: A visual bridge to Markdown conversion
about.description: Bridgex is an open-source graphical interface that simplifies file-to-Markdown conversion. Built on top of Microsoft's Markitdown library, it provides an intuitive visual experience for users who prefer not to use the command line.

whatItDoes.title: What Bridgex Does
items: Converts various file formats to clean Markdown
Provides a graphical interface for Markitdown
Offers a lightweight editor for quick edits
Supports multiple languages in the interface
Works across Windows, macOS, and Linux

whatItDoesNot.title: What Bridgex Does NOT Do
items: Not an IDE or advanced text editor
Not a Markdown editor with live preview
Not a document viewer or PDF reader
Does not perform OCR on image-based PDFs
Not designed for heavy document editing

whyLarge.title: Why is Bridgex Large?
whyLarge.description: Bridgex has a significant file size (90-120MB for the installer) because it bundles:
items: PySide6 (Qt for Python) - Full GUI framework
Markitdown with format plugins - Document parsing
Python runtime libraries - Required for execution

licenses.title: Third-Party Licenses
licenses.subtitle: Bridgex uses the following open-source libraries

markitdown.title: Markitdown
markitdown.description: Document to Markdown conversion library by Microsoft
